2	ddx:
3	  id: plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-011
6	    - FEAT-015
7	    - FEAT-018
8	---
9	# DDx Skill Package Layout
10	
11	Date: 2026-05-13
12	Status: Draft
13	
14	## Summary
15	
16	The DDx skill tree should be content owned by the default `ddx` plugin package,
17	not a parallel bootstrap tree with checked-in mirrors. `ddx init`, `ddx update`,
18	and `ddx plugin install ddx` should all install the same package layout through
19	the same package installer.
20	
21	Development installs may use symlink overlays for live editing, but those
22	overlays are project-local. DDx must not restore home-directory skill installs;
23	the only expected home binary is `~/.local/bin/ddx`.
24	
25	## Current Problems
26	
27	- `skills/ddx/` is the editable source, but `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`,
28	  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, `.claude/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` are
29	  full copied mirrors.
30	- `cli/Makefile copy-skills` syncs the top-level skill tree into embedded and
31	  project-local copies, so editing behavior depends on remembering which mirror
32	  is canonical.
33	- `ddx init` installs shipped skills through a direct bootstrap path using
34	  `skills.SkillFiles`, then separately installs the default `ddx` plugin
35	  package.
36	- `ddx update` refreshes shipped skills through the same direct embedded-skill
37	  path rather than the package installer.
38	- `ddx plugin install ddx --local .` is unsafe in the current repo layout
39	  because local install prefers `<pluginRoot>/.agents/skills` before
40	  `<pluginRoot>/skills`; pointed at the repo root, that can target the
41	  destination skill tree instead of the intended source.
42	
43	## Target Layout
44	
45	The default package owns the DDx skill:
46	
47	```text
48	library/
49	  package.yaml
50	  skills/
51	    ddx/
52	      SKILL.md
53	      reference/
54	      bead-lifecycle/
55	      ...
56	```
57	
58	The checked-in editable source is `library/skills/ddx/`.
59	
60	The release binary embeds the default package content, not a special skill-only
61	tree:
62	
63	```text
64	library/ -> cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/
65	```
66	
67	The project-local discovered skill paths are install outputs:
68	
69	```text
70	.agents/skills/ddx  -> installed by package installer
71	.claude/skills/ddx  -> installed by package installer
72	```
73	
74	For registry installs, these outputs are real files. For local development
75	overlays, they may be symlinks to the source checkout.
76	
77	## Manifest Contract
78	
79	`library/package.yaml` declares package-local skill sources:
80	
81	```yaml
82	install:
83	  root:
84	    source: .
85	    target: .ddx/plugins/ddx
86	  skills:
87	    - source: skills/
88	      target: .agents/skills/
89	    - source: skills/
90	      target: .claude/skills/
91	```
92	
93	The installer must honor `install.skills[*].source` relative to the package
94	root. Skill discovery must not hard-code `.agents/skills` as the primary source
95	when the manifest explicitly says `skills/`.
96	
97	Directory-to-directory skill mappings install the child skill directories under
98	the target. The example above installs `skills/ddx` to `.agents/skills/ddx` and
99	`.claude/skills/ddx`; it does not replace `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills`
100	as whole directories. This keeps unrelated project skills visible.
101	
102	## Installer Contract
103	
104	Split the package installer into source-agnostic operations:
105	
106	```go
107	InstallPackageFromRemote(pkg, projectRoot)
108	InstallPackageFromDir(pkg, sourceDir, projectRoot)
109	InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, sourceFS, projectRoot)
110	```
111	
112	All three paths must share the same core install implementation:
113	
114	1. load and validate `package.yaml` when present;
115	2. apply `install.root.source -> install.root.target`;
116	3. apply every `install.skills[*].source -> install.skills[*].target`;
117	4. apply scripts/executables with the existing project-scope rules;
118	5. return one `InstalledEntry` shape.
119	
120	Remote and embedded installs write real files. Local installs create developer
121	overlays: `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and plugin-owned skill outputs are symlinks to
122	the local checkout. Local overlays do not mutate recorded plugin pins and do not
123	auto-commit.
124	
125	## Init And Update Contract
126	
127	`ddx init` installs the default DDx package through the embedded package
128	installer. It no longer calls a separate embedded skill installer and no longer
129	maintains `.ddx/skills/ddx` as a bootstrap-only mirror.
130	
131	`ddx update` / the forward replacement for shipped-content refresh uses the
132	same package installer path as `ddx init`.
133	
134	Remove these direct bootstrap mechanisms after replacement tests exist:
135	
136	- `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)` calls from `ddx init` and update;
137	- `registerBootstrapDDxSkills`;
138	- `cli/internal/skills/ddx` as a distinct embedded source;
139	- `skills/ddx` as the top-level canonical source;
140	- checked-in `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx`
141	  mirrors.
142	
143	## Development Workflow
144	
145	The repo development workflow becomes:
146	
147	```bash
148	ddx plugin install ddx --local library --force
149	```
150	
151	After that command, edits to `library/skills/ddx/` are live through the
152	project-local harness discovery paths.
153	
154	The command must be safe when run from the DDx repo. If someone passes the repo
155	root instead of `library`, the installer must either resolve to the manifest's
156	declared package root or fail with a clear error; it must not self-link
157	`.agents/skills/ddx`.
158	
159	## Migration Sequence
160	
161	1. Add embedded default-plugin package support for `library/`.
162	2. Teach the installer to honor `install.skills[*].source` for remote,
163	   embedded, and local installs.
164	3. Change `ddx init` to install `ddx` through the embedded package installer.
165	4. Change shipped-content refresh/update to use the same package installer.
166	5. Move `skills/ddx/` to `library/skills/ddx/`.
167	6. Replace `copy-skills` with a default-package embed sync:
168	   `library/ -> cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/`.
169	7. Remove the checked-in skill mirrors after tests prove install outputs are
170	   produced by init/local install.
171	8. Update FEAT-011, FEAT-015, SD-011, and any command help or AGENTS blocks that
172	   describe copied bootstrap skill mirrors.
173	
174	## Required Tests
175	
176	- `TestInitInstallsDDxPluginPackage`: `ddx init` creates `.ddx/plugins/ddx`,
177	  `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` through the package installer.
178	- `TestPluginInstallLocalDDxLibrarySymlinksSkills`: `ddx plugin install ddx
179	  --local library --force` creates project-local symlinks to
180	  `library/skills/ddx`.
181	- `TestPluginInstallHonorsSkillSource`: a fixture with `install.skills.source:
182	  skills/` installs skills from `skills/` and does not require
183	  `.agents/skills` inside the source package.
184	- `TestPluginInstallPreservesUnrelatedProjectSkills`: installing the `ddx`
185	  package does not replace `.agents/skills` or `.claude/skills` when other
186	  skills already exist there.
187	- `TestInitWorksOfflineWithEmbeddedDefaultPlugin`: no network is needed for
188	  `ddx init` to install the default package and skill.
189	- `TestRepoRootLocalInstallDoesNotSelfLink`: passing the DDx repo root either
190	  resolves to the package root safely or fails before modifying skill outputs.
191	- `ddx skills check library/skills/ddx .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx`
192	  passes after local install.
193	- `make skill-schema` validates the package-owned skill source and embedded
194	  default-package copy.
195	
196	## Non-Scope
197	
198	- Do not restore home-directory skill installs.
199	- Do not change the public plugin CLI names beyond the separate plugin CLI
200	  rationalization plan.
201	- Do not remove the embedded package copy until offline `ddx init` is green.
202

1	# DDx CLI Makefile
2	
3	# Build variables
4	VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
5	COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
6	DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
7	LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"
8	
9	# Build directory
10	BUILD_DIR = build
11	
12	# Platforms to build for
13	PLATFORMS = \
14		darwin/amd64 \
15		darwin/arm64 \
16		linux/amd64 \
17		linux/arm64 \
18		windows/amd64
19	
20	.PHONY: all build clean test test-full test-perf lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build frontend-rebuild frontend-verify copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql
21	
22	# Default target
23	all: clean deps test build
24	
25	# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
26	# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
27	FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
28	FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/.frontend-build-stamp
29	
30	frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)
31	
32	$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
33		@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
34		cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
35		@touch internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep $(FRONTEND_STAMP)
36	
37	# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
38	frontend-rebuild:
39		@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
40		cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
41		@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify
42	
43	# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
44	# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
45	# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
46	# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
47	# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
48	frontend-verify:
49		@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
50		@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
51		missing=0; \
52		for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
53			path=".$$ref"; \
54			if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
55				echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
56				missing=$$((missing+1)); \
57			fi; \
58		done; \
59		if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
60			echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
61			exit 1; \
62		fi; \
63		echo "  ok"
64	
65	# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
66	# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
67	# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
68	# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
69	# Also refreshes project-local copies (.agents, .claude, .ddx) when they
70	# already exist, so interactive-behavior edits stay in sync across all five
71	# shipped paths without a separate ddx init/update invocation.
72	SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
73	SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
74	copy-skills:
75		@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
76			echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
77		fi
78		@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/
79		@if [ -d "../.agents/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.agents/skills/ddx/; fi
80		@if [ -d "../.claude/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.claude/skills/ddx/; fi
81		@if [ -d "../.ddx/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.ddx/skills/ddx/; fi
82	
83	# Build for current platform
84	build: copy-skills frontend-build frontend-verify
85		@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
86		@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
87		go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
88	
89	# Build for all platforms
90	build-all: clean deps copy-skills
91		@echo "Building for all platforms..."
92		@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
93		@echo "Building current platform..."
94		go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
95		@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
96			OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
97			ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
98			echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
99			GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
100			if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
101				mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
102			fi; \
103		done
104	
105	# Install dependencies
106	deps:
107		@echo "Installing dependencies..."
108		go mod download
109		go mod tidy
110	
111	# Run tests
112	test: copy-skills
113		@echo "Running tests..."
114		go test -v ./...
115	
116	# Run full test suite with race detector and coverage (mirrors CI; opt-in for local pre-push verification)
117	test-full: copy-skills
118		@echo "Running full test suite (race + coverage)..."
119		for var in $$(git rev-parse --local-env-vars 2>/dev/null); do unset "$$var"; done; \
120		CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race -cover ./...
121	
122	# Run the GraphQL perf harness (ddx-9ce6842a). Seeds a 10 × 500 bead fixture
123	# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override
124	# the fixture size or the report date via BENCH_REPORT_DATE.
125	BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)
126	BENCH_REPORT_DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
127	bench-graphql:
128		@echo "Running GraphQL perf harness (fixture: 10 × 500 beads = 5,000)..."
129		@mkdir -p $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)
130		DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR=$(BENCH_REPORT_DIR) \
131		DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DATE=$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE) \
132		go test -tags=perf ./internal/server/perf/ -run TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline -v -timeout 5m
133		@echo "Baseline report written to $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)/$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE)-baseline.md"
134	
135	# Run perf tests (excluded from default `make test`; use this target or CI perf job)
136	test-perf:
137		@echo "Running perf tests (excluded from default test run)..."
138		go test -tags=perf ./internal/server/perf/... -v -timeout 5m
139	
140	# Validate the shared bead record schema
141	bead-schema:
142		@echo "Validating bead record schema..."
143		go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestBeadRecordSchema' -count=1
144	
145	# Validate bundled skill metadata
146	skill-schema:
147		@echo "Validating bundled skill metadata..."
148		go run . skills check

152		@echo "Running linter..."
153		@if command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
154			golangci-lint run; \
155		else \
156			echo "golangci-lint not found, running go vet instead"; \

158		fi

160		@go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...
161

163	install: build

166

169		@echo "Cleaning..."
170		rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)
171	
172	# Run the CLI (for development)
173	run:
174		go run . $(ARGS)
175	
176	# Format code
177	fmt:
178		go fmt ./...
179	
180	# Update dependencies
181	update-deps:
182		go get -u all
183		go mod tidy
184

190			if [[ $$file == *.exe ]]; then \

14	//go:embed all:library
15	var embedded embed.FS
16	
17	// FS returns a filesystem rooted at the embedded default-plugin package
18	// (the equivalent of the on-disk `library/` directory). The returned fs.FS
19	// is suitable for passing to registry.InstallPackageFromFS.
20	func FS() fs.FS {
21		sub, err := fs.Sub(embedded, "library")
22		if err != nil {
23			// embed roots are verified at compile time; a sub failure means the
24			// embedded tree shape changed under us.
25			panic(err)
26		}
27		return sub
28	}

145	func newPackageArchiveServer(t *testing.T, payload []byte) *httptest.Server {
146		t.Helper()
147	
148		return httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
149			if !strings.HasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/archive/refs/tags/v1.0.0.tar.gz") {
150				t.Errorf("unexpected archive path: %s", r.URL.Path)
151			}
152			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/gzip")
153			_, _ = w.Write(payload)
154		}))
155	}

158		t.Helper()
159	
160		require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
161			Name:     filepath.ToSlash(name) + "/",

164		}))

168		t.Helper()
169

173			Size:     int64(len(body)),
174			Typeflag: tar.TypeReg,
175		}))
176		_, err := tw.Write(body)
177		require.NoError(t, err)
178	}

48			".ddx/plugins/ddx/package.yaml must be present after install")
49	
50		// Skill outputs installed via install.skills[*] mappings.

52			skillDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, target, "skills", "ddx")

54			assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md"),
55				"%s/skills/ddx/SKILL.md must be installed from the embedded package", target)
56		}
57	}

# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
# Also refreshes project-local copies (.agents, .claude, .ddx) when they
# already exist, so interactive-behavior edits stay in sync across all five
# shipped paths without a separate ddx init/update invocation.
SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/
	@if [ -d "../.agents/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.agents/skills/ddx/; fi
	@if [ -d "../.claude/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.claude/skills/ddx/; fi
	@if [ -d "../.ddx/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.ddx/skills/ddx/; fi

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:241-258 sends the policy/profile request to Fizeau and correctly leaves concrete provider selection to Fizeau, but DDx does not add any negative constraint or route-failure feedback after the selected provider fails.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a route-failure feedback path for provider connectivity failures. When a service-backed attempt fails before producing agent output because the selected provider endpoint timed out or was unreachable, DDx should record structured route failure evidence and retry the same bead with the same operator intent while avoiding the exact failed route when Fizeau supports that constraint, or by raising the fallback floor/policy only after the cheap/free route has actually failed. The retry must not hardcode provider names or policy names.

NON-SCOPE
